segments.text: but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.
Now very early in the morning, he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him. He sat down and taught them.
The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman taken in adultery. Having set her in the middle,
they told him,
Teacher, we found this woman in adultery, in the very act.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now in our law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. What then do you say about her?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They said this testing him, that they might have something to accuse him of. But Jesus stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
But when they continued asking him, he looked up and said to them,
He who is without sin among you, let him throw the first stone at her.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Again he stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
They, when they heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning from the oldest, even to the last. Jesus was left alone with the woman where she was, in the middle.
Jesus, standing up, saw her and said,
Woman, where are your accusers? Did no one condemn you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: She said,
No one, Lord.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said,
Neither do I condemn you. Go your way. From now on, sin no more.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying,
I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Pharisees therefore said to him,
You testify about yourself. Your testimony is not valid.
segments.speaker: The Pharisees therefore

segments.text: Jesus answered them,
Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from, and where I am going; but you don’t know where I came from, or where I am going.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You judge according to the flesh. I judge no one.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It’s also written in your law that the testimony of two people is valid.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said therefore to him,
Where is your Father?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered,
You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus spoke these words in the treasury, as he taught in the temple. Yet no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.
Jesus said therefore again to them,
I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins. Where I go, you can’t come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Jews therefore said,
Will he kill himself, because he says,‘Where I am going, you can’t come’?
segments.speaker: The Jews therefore

segments.text: He said to them,
You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world.
segments.speaker: The Jews therefore

segments.text: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.
segments.speaker: The Jews therefore

segments.text: They said therefore to him,
Who are you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to them,
Just what I have been saying to you from the beginning.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you. However, he who sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these I say to the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They didn’t understand that he spoke to them about the Father.
Jesus therefore said to them,
When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn’t left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As he spoke these things, many believed in him.
Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him,
If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They answered him,
We are Abraham’s offspring, and have never been in bondage to anyone. How do you say,‘You will be made free’?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus answered them,
Most certainly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A bondservant doesn’t live in the house forever. A son remains forever.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I know that you are Abraham’s offspring, yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I say the things which I have seen with my Father; and you also do the things which you have seen with your father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They answered him,
Our father is Abraham.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to them,
If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth which I heard from God. Abraham didn’t do this.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You do the works of your father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They said to him,
We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father, God.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore Jesus said to them,
If God were your father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from God. For I haven’t come of myself, but he sent me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why don’t you understand my speech? Because you can’t hear my word.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You are of your father the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and doesn’t st and in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks on his own; for he is a liar, and the father of lies.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But because I tell the truth, you don’t believe me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He who is of God hears the words of God. For this cause you don’t hear, because you are not of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the Jews answered him,
Don’t we say well that you are a Samaritan, and have a demon?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered,
I don’t have a demon, but I honor my Father and you dishonor me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Most certainly, I tell you, if a person keeps my word, he will never see death.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the Jews said to him,
Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, as did the prophets; and you say,‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets died. Who do you make yourself out to be?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Jesus answered,
If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say that he is our God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, ‘I don’t know him,’ I would be like you, a liar. But I know him and keep his word.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day. He saw it and was glad.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Jews therefore said to him,
You are not yet fifty years old! Have you seen Abraham?
segments.speaker: The Jews therefore

segments.text: Jesus said to them,
Most certainly, I tell you, before Abraham came into existence, I AM.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore they took up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple, having gone through the middle of them, and so passed by.
